name: Walnut Chicken/Shrimp Stir-Fry
recipeIngredient: 1.5 pounds Shrimp or chicken breast, cut into strips
3 small heads Chinese cabbage, sliced crosswise into 1/8-inch-thick slices
2 large Onions, halved and thinly sliced
1 pound Snow peas or sugar snaps
1 can (8 oz) Sliced water chestnuts, drained
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, boil walnut halves in enough water to cover for 3 minutes; drain and set aside.
2. In a small bowl, combine soy sauce, cornstarch, salt, and sugar. Stir well. Add shrimp or chicken strips and toss to coat. Set aside.
3. Heat peanut oil over medium-high heat in a large wok or skillet. Add all vegetables and stir-fry until tender-crisp, about 3 minutes. Remove the vegetables and set aside.
4. Add walnut halves to the wok and sauté until lightly browned. If necessary, add more oil for even cooking.
5. Add water chestnuts and sauté for 1-2 minutes longer. Combine with reserved vegetables and set aside.
6. Heat remaining oil in a separate pan over high heat. Remove shrimp or chicken from the marinade, allowing excess to drip off. Set aside the marinade.
7. Stir-fry shrimp or chicken strips until cooked through, about 3-4 minutes.
8. Add chicken broth and reserved marinade to the wok. Cook until bubbling. Return the vegetables, walnut halves, and water chestnuts to the pan.
9. Toss everything together and stir in tarragon. Serve immediately over steamed rice or crisp noodles.
